decisions.title: Field/relationship/constraint authority moves to structured, validated, introspection-checked JSON; domain-model.md stays prose
decisions.context: model-surface-audit.md is CLAUDE.md's designated field/relationship/constraint authority and is routed-to by adjacent work (the two envisioning docs + the gaps catalogue + domain-model.md, which defers field-authority to it). It drifted on DEC-45 (still asserts the deleted MissionArea, the removed mission-area promotion gate, Plan.guiding_clauses). reference-data-spec.md drifted undetected through the pre-DEC-45 Department->Division and SubjectKind->ImprovementType renames (DISC-40). A hand-maintained prose field-authority is therefore a standing drift liability. Per the DEC-41 framing the real authority is the code — the models, grounding.SECTION_KEYS, and the parsers (the parser is the schema validator). The open question is where field/relationship/constraint authority should live so it cannot silently drift.
decisions.decision: Direction (not yet settled): field/relationship/constraint authority moves to structured, schema-validated JSON, ideally generated-from or checked-against the live code so it cannot drift silently (e.g. a registered lens-validator that introspects Django _meta + grounding.SECTION_KEYS + parser keys and fails context-validate on divergence). domain-model.md stays PROSE — it carries meaning/concepts (why mvv, what a clause is), which is not introspectable structure. model-surface-audit.md is demoted to a dated snapshot / derived artifact once the JSON authority exists, with its citers (CLAUDE.md's authority pointer, the two envisioning docs, the gaps catalogue) repointed.

options_considered.label: Generated / checked JSON authority
options_considered.description: The field-surface JSON is generated from, or verified against, live-code introspection by a registered validator.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Drift-proof and queryable, but the validator's registration is framework-side (barrel import), not project-local config; and a new invariant class needs source changes, so the check must be a lens-validator.

options_considered.label: Curated JSON authority (hand-maintained)
options_considered.description: A typed JSON block holds the field surface, edited by hand on model changes.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Queryable and schema-shaped, but re-drifts exactly as the prose did — format is not what fixes drift.

options_considered.label: Code-only (no prose or JSON mirror)
options_considered.description: Readers introspect the models directly; no maintained authority artifact.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Lowest maintenance and cannot drift, but least narratable/queryable for planning.

consequences: domain-model.md's mvv concept correction is the high-leverage forward doc and stays prose.
model-surface-audit.md becomes a dated snapshot or a derived artifact; CLAUDE.md's authority pointer and the envisioning-doc + gaps-catalogue citers are repointed at the new authority.
The drift-check (an introspection lens-validator) is the piece that makes JSON authority non-drifting; without it, JSON is the same trap in a nicer format.
Open sub-forks remain: which of the three options; and whether model-surface-audit.md is demoted vs corrected-in-place.

references.label: DEC-41 (workshop=dev-mode framing; the parser is the schema validator)
